article_meta.title: close Video American Accountability Foundation president releases Education Department 'watch list,' explains why Trump

reasoning: The model has several issues: attribution rule violations (flagging quoted/attributed text), incorrect bias type labels (using labels not in the defined list), and some defensible but questionable identifications. Let me assess each item.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Item 1: 'Biden's Book Ban Hoax' - This phrase appears in the author's prose summarizing the department's announcement, but it's clearly paraphrasing/relaying the DOE's own characterization. Borderline. Item 2: 'the top bureaucrat ideologues' - This is from the video description/headline attributing Tom Jones's words, not author prose. Attribution violation. Item 3: 'restoring the fundamental rights of parents...' - This is explicitly inside quotation marks from Craig Trainor. Clear attribution violation. Item 4: 'meritless' and 'dubious legal theory' are in quotation marks attributed to DOE. Attribution violation. Multiple items flag attributed language.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Items 2, 3, and 4 are all flagging attributed/quoted language as author bias - these are false positives in that they misidentify the source of the language. Item 1 is partially defensible as author framing but still largely relays DOE language. The majority of flagged items are not genuine author-level bias.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed some genuine author-prose bias: the use of 'so-called book bans' in author prose (dismissive framing), the structural choice to present the story almost entirely from the DOE/administration perspective (Slant), and phrases like 'working to protect students from obscene content' which frames the parents' side positively in author prose. The article also has bias by story choice/placement issues. Some were missed.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The model used 'Loaded Language', 'Labeling', 'One-sided Reporting', and 'Selective Omission' - none of which are in the defined bias type list. The correct labels would be 'Word Choice', 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives', 'Slant', and 'Bias by Omission' respectively. All four labels are from outside the provided list.
